key_insight: CO₂ laser welding of 2 mm Strenx 700 CR steel can produce sound joints with a narrow heat-affected zone, but the weld microstructure changes substantially toward martensite/bainite-rich regions that influence strength and fatigue.
engineering_relevance: For lightweight agricultural machinery, laser welding offers a faster, lower-distortion alternative to conventional arc welding while preserving high-strength steel performance in cyclic loading applications.
method: Autogenous CO₂ laser welds on 2 mm Strenx 700 sheets
Compared three power levels: 1100, 1300, and 1500 W
Characterized welds using microscopy, microhardness mapping, tensile tests, and tension-tension fatigue testing
evidence_signal: The abstract reports satisfactory macro-weld formation and detailed phase identification across the fusion zone, coarse-grained HAZ, fine-grained HAZ, and base metal, alongside mechanical and fatigue evaluation.
watch_item: Because the study used manual autogenous welds on thin sheet, results may be sensitive to parameter control and may not directly translate to thicker sections or production-scale welding.
paper_title: Effects of Laser Welding on the Microstructure and Fatigue Behavior of Strenx 700 CR Steel
